text: Aerobic performance is degraded in cold environments, though the extent has not been thoroughly studied.
Decreases in deep body temperature and muscle temperature are key physiological mechanisms behind aerobic performance degradation in cold conditions.
Strength and power output are degraded during cold exposure, primarily due to declines in muscle temperature.
Cold tolerance is influenced by habituation, anthropometry, sex, race, and aerobic fitness level.
Thermoregulatory fatigue — a reduction in shivering and vasoconstriction responses — can occur as a result of exhaustive exercise combined with cold exposure.
Cold shock from short-term cold water immersion produces acute physiological and pathophysiological responses distinct from longer-term cold air exposure.
